questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The report ____ serious concerns about safety.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I would rather prefer tea than coffee.
options: I would rather tea than coffee.
I would rather have tea than coffee.
I would rather prefer tea than coffee.
I prefer tea rather than coffee.
questions.solution: I would rather have tea than coffee.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The new regulation ____ into effect next month.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The police has arrested the suspect last night.
options: The police have arrested the suspect last night.
The police arrested the suspect last night.
The police has arrested the suspect last night.
The police had arrested the suspect last night.
questions.solution: The police arrested the suspect last night.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The evidence ____ against the accused.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The price of these goods have increased.
options: The price of these goods has increased.
The price of these goods are increased.
The prices of these goods has increased.
The price of these goods have increased.
questions.solution: The price of these goods has increased.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: He is ____ to the needs of the poor.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He asked me to convey him my regards.
options: He asked that I convey him my regards.
He asked me to convey to him my regards.
He asked me to convey my regards to him.
He asked me to convey him my regards.
questions.solution: He asked me to convey to him my regards.

questions.stem: The scientist ____ the experiment with great precision.

questions.stem: Correct: I prefer coffee than tea.

questions.stem: The findings were ____ in a leading journal.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Neither the manager nor his assistants was present.
options: Neither the manager nor his assistants are being present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants has been present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants were present.
Neither the manager nor his assistants is present.
questions.solution: Neither the manager nor his assistants were present.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The situation is ____ by the delay in approvals.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The team is working on the project since May.
options: The team had been working on the project since May.
The team was working on the project since May.
The team has worked on the project since May.
The team has been working on the project since May.
questions.solution: The team has been working on the project since May.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The scenery of the mountains are breathtaking.
options: The sceneries of the mountains are breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains are breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains is breathtaking.
The scenery of the mountains have been breathtaking.
questions.solution: The scenery of the mountains is breathtaking.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Maxwell's equations describe:
options: Relationship between electric and magnetic fields
questions.solution: Maxwell: Gauss (E), Gauss (M), Faraday, Ampere-Maxwell. Basis of EM theory.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A BJT with β = 150 has a base current of 20 µA. Its collector current is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Einstein relation: D/μ = kT/q = V_T. At 300K:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stack operations in 8085:
options: PUSH decrements SP by 2, POP increments SP by 2
questions.solution: Stack grows downward. PUSH: SP←SP-2, then store. POP: read, then SP←SP+2.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Buck converter CCM/DCM boundary: occurs when:
options: Inductor current reaches zero at switching instant
questions.solution: CCM→DCM boundary: I_L(min) = 0. Below boundary, inductor current pulses. Efficiency and dynamics change.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: IIR filter stability: all poles must be:
questions.solution: Stability: |pole| < 1 for causal IIR. On unit circle = marginally stable. Outside = unstable. Key difference from FIR (always stable).

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Skin depth: decreases with:
questions.solution: δ = 1/√(πfμσ). Higher f → thinner skin depth. Higher σ or μ also reduce δ. Current confined to surface.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Flash memory programming: must be done by:
questions.solution: Flash: requires erase (block) before write (page). Programming requires specific command sequences and voltage.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: CRO deflection sensitivity: defined as:
questions.solution: Sensitivity: mm/V or div/V. Higher sensitivity = larger deflection for same voltage. Vertical amplifier setting.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Differential amplifier rejects:
questions.solution: CMRR = |A_d/A_cm|. High CMRR = good rejection of noise common to both inputs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Wave impedance of plane wave in lossy medium:
questions.solution: Lossy: Z is complex. sigma/(omega*eps) >> 1: good conductor. << 1: good dielectric.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inverting amplifier: Rf=47kΩ, Rin=1kΩ. Voltage gain:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular waveguide supports:
questions.solution: Waveguide: TE_mn (E_z=0) and TM_mn (H_z=0). No TEM (needs two conductors). Cutoff frequency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Difference amplifier output: Vout = (Rf/R₁)(V₂-V₁) when all R equal:
questions.solution: Difference amp: V_out = (Rf/R₁)(V₂-V₁). For all equal R: V₂-V₁.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: PLL — Phase Locked Loop:
options: VCO + phase detector + loop filter. Locks VCO to input frequency and phase
questions.solution: PLL: capture range > lock range. 565 PLL. FM demodulation, frequency synthesis, clock recovery.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The speed of light in free space:
questions.solution: c = 3×10⁸ m/s. In medium: v = c/√(ε_r μ_r).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A flip-flop is the basic building block of:
questions.solution: Flip-flops store state and form registers, counters and other sequential logic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The 8085 microprocessor has:
options: 8-bit data bus, 16-bit address bus (64 KB memory)

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Smith chart normalizes impedance to characteristic impedance Z₀. Center of chart represents:
questions.solution: Smith chart center: z = Z/Z₀ = 1 (matched). Edge: reflection coefficient = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Encoder:
options: Converts active input line to binary code (n inputs → log₂n outputs)
questions.solution: Encoder: 8:3, 10:4 (decimal to BCD). Priority encoder: handles simultaneous inputs.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RESET signal in 8085 clears:
options: PC to 0000H and sets stack pointer to FFFFH (by convention)
questions.solution: RESET: PC←0000H. SP undefined (but BIOS sets it). Interrupts disabled.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Array factor of N-element uniform linear array with spacing d and phase δ:
questions.solution: AF = Σ_{n=0}^{N-1} e^{jnψ}. Simplifies to ratio of sines. Max when ψ=0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which gate set is NOT functionally complete on its own?
questions.solution: NAND or NOR alone are universal; AND alone cannot realize all functions (no inversion).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CALL instruction pushes:
questions.solution: CALL: push PC+2 (return addr), then jump to subroutine. RET pops return address.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cavity resonator Q-factor is typically:
questions.solution: Cavity: high Q (10³-10⁶). Waveguide cavities used in filters, oscillators.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which gate gives an output of 1 only when both inputs differ?
questions.solution: XOR outputs 1 when the two inputs are unequal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transfer function G(s)H(s) = K/(s(s+1)(s+2)). Number of asymptotes in root locus:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 13 kHz tone is sampled at 10 kHz. The alias frequency that appears in the baseband is:
questions.solution: Alias = |f − fs| = |13 − 10| = 3 kHz (since f exceeds fs).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An FM signal has peak deviation 10 kHz and message bandwidth 15 kHz. By Carson's rule the bandwidth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Region of Convergence (ROC) for a right-sided sequence is:
questions.solution: Causal (right-sided): ROC outside outermost pole. Anti-causal: inside innermost pole. Two-sided: annulus.

questions.solution: Schmitt trigger: positive feedback. V_UTP and V_LTP. Noise immunity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Karnaugh map — simplification:
options: Group adjacent 1s (powers of 2). Eliminate variables that change within group
questions.solution: K-map: 2, 3, 4, 5 variables. Wrap-around edges. Don't care conditions: X.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Modulation index m in AM:
options: m = V_m/V_c (ratio of modulating to carrier amplitude)
questions.solution: m must be ≤ 1 to avoid overmodulation (distortion). m > 1 → envelope distortion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: JFET vs BJT input impedance:
questions.solution: JFET: gate reverse biased. BJT: base-emitter forward biased.

options: Frequency Division. Different carriers for different channels. Guard bands

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: OFDM (Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing):
questions.solution: OFDM: used in WiFi (802.11a/g/n/ac), 4G/5G, DVB. Subcarriers orthogonal → no ICI. FFT-based.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Negative resistance oscillator: oscillation condition:
questions.solution: Barkhausen criterion: loop gain = 1 with 0° phase. Negative resistance cancels tank loss for sustained oscillation.

questions.stem: Feedback types:
questions.solution: Negative feedback: reduces gain, increases bandwidth, stabilizes, reduces distortion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Feedback effect on output impedance:
options: Voltage sampling: ↓ Rout. Current sampling: ↑ Rout
questions.solution: Voltage feedback: stabilizes Vout (low Rout). Current feedback: stabilizes Iout (high Rout).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Miller capacitance: C_M = C(1-A_v). For C=10pF, A_v=-100:
questions.solution: C_in = C(1+|A_v|) = 10×101 = 1010 pF. Miller effect multiplies capacitance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Switching regulators — buck converter:
options: V_out = D·V_in. D = duty cycle. Inductor + capacitor filter. High efficiency (>85%)
questions.solution: Buck: step-down. PWM control. Sync rectification for higher efficiency. CCM vs DCM.

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Gilbert cell multiplier: uses:
questions.solution: Gilbert cell: 4-quadrant multiplier using cross-coupled differential pairs. Double-balanced: suppresses LO and RF feedthrough.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Class B amplifier efficiency maximum is:
questions.solution: Class B: η_max = π/4 ≈ 78.5%. Class A: 25%. Class AB: between A and B.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VCO (Voltage Controlled Oscillator):
options: Output frequency ∝ input voltage. 555 timer, LM566, VCO in PLL
questions.solution: VCO: tuning voltage. f_out = k·V_ctrl. VCO gain K_v (Hz/V). Used in PLL.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Maxwell's equations predict that a time-varying electric field produces a:
questions.solution: Faraday/Ampère–Maxwell coupling of changing E and B fields gives rise to EM waves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In amplitude modulation (AM), the quantity varied in proportion to the message is the carrier's:
questions.solution: AM varies the carrier amplitude with the modulating signal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An n-bit ADC/DAC provides how many distinct output levels?
questions.solution: n bits quantize into 2ⁿ levels; resolution = full-scale/2ⁿ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A unit step function u(t) is:
questions.solution: u(t): sudden change at t=0. Used to represent switching signals.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Nyquist stability: encirclements of -1 point give:
questions.solution: N = P - Z. Encirclements of -1 (clockwise) = Z = P - N. Z must be 0 for stability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Delta modulation (DM):
options: Transmits 1-bit per sample (up/down step based on signal slope)
questions.solution: DM: simple, low rate. Slope overload (step too small for fast signal). Granular noise (step too large for flat signal).

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: DSSS synchronization: acquisition stage finds:
questions.solution: Acquisition: coarse alignment of spreading code. Tracking: fine alignment and maintenance. Uses correlators.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metastability in flip-flops occurs when:
options: Setup or hold time violations cause indeterminate output
questions.solution: Metastability: output oscillates or stays between 0 and 1. MTBF decreases with clock frequency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RC low-pass: R=1kΩ, C=0.01μF. -3dB frequency:

questions.subject: Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering
questions.stem: Spread spectrum processing gain:
options: Bandwidth of spread signal / data bandwidth
questions.solution: PG = BWspread / BWdata. Provides interference rejection. Higher PG → more resistant to jamming/narrowband interference.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Digital modulation — FSK:
options: Frequency shift keying. Two frequencies for 0 and 1. Non-coherent detection possible
questions.solution: FSK: Bell 103 (300 baud). MSK (minimum shift): continuous phase. GMSK in GSM.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A signal has a maximum frequency of 10 kHz. Its Nyquist sampling rate is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Binary half adder outputs:
questions.solution: Half adder: S = A⊕B, C = A·B. No carry input.
